title: OmniQuery Pipeline
description: Defines an OmniQuery ETL pipeline: load data from sources (loaders), transform via SQL in DuckDB, and export to destinations (outputs).

name.description: Pipeline identifier (shown in logs and in --dry-run).

parameters.description: Dynamic parameters substituted from the CLI as --param_name value, referenced in YAML as {{ param_name }}.

loads.description: List of data sources loaded into DuckDB before the outputs run.

outputs.description: List of destinations where the transformed data is written.

name.description: Parameter name — used as --param_name on the CLI and {{ param_name }} in YAML.

type.description: Expected value type on the CLI.

description.description: Text shown in the CLI --help for this parameter.

required.description: If true, the CLI requires this parameter to be supplied (no default).

default.description: Default value when the parameter is not supplied on the CLI.

table.description: Table loaded into DuckDB — the alias is the name used by the output queries.

alias.description: Table name in DuckDB. Must be a valid SQL identifier.

type.description: Content kind: 'inline' for SQL inside the YAML, 'sql' for a path to an external .sql file.

content.description: SQL query (when type: inline) or path to a .sql file (when type: sql). Supports {{ parameters }}.
examples: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE date >= '{{ start_date }}'

if_exists.description: Behavior if the table already exists in DuckDB.

loader.description: Data source loaded into DuckDB. Use 'type: database' for databases through SQLAlchemy, or 'type: file' for CSV/Excel files.

database_loader.description: Load data from a relational database (PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle) into DuckDB.

source.description: Database name as defined in databases.yaml.

tables.description: Tables to extract from the database and load into DuckDB.

file_loader.description: Load data from a CSV, XLSX, or XLS file into DuckDB.

source.description: Path to the input file (.csv, .xlsx, or .xls).

tables.description: Tables produced from the file in DuckDB.

output.description: Destination for the transformed data. Use 'type: database' to write to a database, or 'type: file' to export to a file.

database_output.description: Export data from DuckDB to a destination database.

name.description: Destination table name. Supports schema-qualified names: 'schema.table'.

query.description: SQL query executed against DuckDB to produce the output data. References the aliases declared in `loads`.

options.description: Write options for the destination database.

if_exists.description: 'replace' recreates the table from scratch; 'append' adds rows without dropping.

file_output.description: Export data from DuckDB to a CSV, XLSX, or XLS file (format inferred from the extension).

name.description: Output file path. Format is inferred from the extension (.csv, .xlsx, .xls).

query.description: SQL query executed against DuckDB to produce the file data.
examples: SELECT * FROM sales ORDER BY issue_date

options.description: Write options for the output file.

if_exists.description: 'replace' overwrites the file; 'append' adds rows (CSV only).
